name: Georgia Peach Pie
recipeInstructions:
Prepare Crust: In a large bowl, combine the all-purpose flour and salt. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Sprinkle 5 to 6 tablespoons of cold water, 1 tablespoon at a time, over the flour mixture; toss with a fork to blend lightly but evenly. When the mixture forms a ball, gently knead it 2 or 3 times. Divide the dough in half. On a lightly floured surface, roll out one half of the dough to form a 12-inch circle; fit into a 9-inch pie plate; trim, leaving a 1-inch overhang. Roll out the remaining half of the dough on a lightly floured surface into a 12-inch circle; cut with a sharp knife into ten strips, each about 1-inch wide; set aside. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees.
Prepare Filling: In a large bowl, combine the peaches, sugar, 1/4 cup flour, lemon juice, and grated peel; toss well. Pour the filling into the prepared crust. Arrange the reserved dough strips in a lattice pattern over the filling; trim the strips so they match the lower edge of the crust overhang. Lightly dampen the overhang with water. Turn up the ends of the strips and seal. Flute the edge decoratively. Brush the lattice strips and edge of the crust with milk; bake for 50 minutes until bubbling and the crust is golden brown.
